name: WebSocket Position Update (Subscribe/Updates/Unsubscribe)
summary: WebSocket message frames to subscribe to, receive, and unsubscribe from position updates over the Flattrade pi WebSocket (wss://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectWSAPI/). The client sends a subscribe frame (t=p), receives an acknowledgement (t=pk) plus a stream of position-update frames (t=pm) that each carry the full position fields including a child_orders array, and later sends an unsubscribe frame (t=up) which is acknowledged with t=upk.

json_fields.name: t
json_fields.description: Subscribe Position Update REQUEST frame task type. 'p' represents position subscription.

json_fields.name: uid
json_fields.description: Subscribe Position Update REQUEST: User id. (Note: the verbatim subscribe curl sample uses the key "actid" with value "FZ00000" rather than "uid".)

response_fields.name: t (subscribe ack)
response_fields.description: Subscribe Position Update RESPONSE: 'pk' represents position subscription (acknowledgement). On successful connection establishment, position updates will be received if it is made available in the startup (-position_update).

response_fields.name: uid (subscribe ack)
response_fields.description: Subscribe Position Update RESPONSE: User id.

response_fields.name: t (position update)
response_fields.description: POSITION UPDATE SUBSCRIPTION UPDATES frame: 'pm' represents position update.

response_fields.name: child_orders
response_fields.description: Array Object, details given below (see CHILD_ORDERS FORMAT).

response_fields.name: child_orders[].netqty
response_fields.description: CHILD_ORDERS FORMAT: Net Quantity [daybuyqty + cfbuyqty - daysellqty - cfsellqty].

response_fields.name: child_orders[].totbuyavgprc
response_fields.description: CHILD_ORDERS FORMAT: Total Buy Avg Price.

response_fields.name: child_orders[].totsellavgprc
response_fields.description: CHILD_ORDERS FORMAT: Total Sell Avg Price.

response_fields.name: t (unsubscribe req)
response_fields.description: Unsubscribe Position Update REQUEST: 'up' represents Unsubscribe Position update.

response_fields.name: t (unsubscribe ack)
response_fields.description: Unsubscribe Position Update RESPONSE: 'upk' represents Unsubscribe Position update acknowledgement.

notes: This is a WebSocket message-frame flow, not a REST endpoint; all frames use the same socket wss://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectWSAPI/. The 'curl' examples in the doc are illustrative only (the JSON frame is what is sent over the open WebSocket). FLOW: (1) Subscribe REQUEST frame {"t":"p", uid} -> (2) ack frame {"t":"pk", uid} -> (3) stream of position-update frames {"t":"pm", ...full position fields...} where child_orders is an array of per-contract objects (CHILD_ORDERS FORMAT). UNSUBSCRIBE: send {"t":"up"} -> ack {"t":"upk"}. The Unsubscribe REQUEST curl sample sends {"t": "up"} only. Note: position updates are received on connection establishment only if made available in the startup with the -position_update flag. INCONSISTENCY: the Subscribe field table lists 'uid' (User id) but the verbatim subscribe curl sample uses the key "actid":"FZ00000" instead of "uid". Field-name quirks in the doc: 'rpnl' is described verbatim as 'Realized Panel' (i.e. Realized P&L). The HEARTBEAT (t=h / t=hk) and USER DETAILS (POST to /PiConnectAPI/UserDetails) sections that begin on page 83 are SEPARATE sections and are NOT part of this Position Update section; the page-83 Sample Success Response belongs to USER DETAILS, not to Position Update.
issues: No sample success/failure JSON response is shown for the Position Update frames (the page-83 Sample Success Response belongs to the separate USER DETAILS section, not to Position Update). The subscribe field table shows 'uid' but the curl sample uses 'actid' — documentation inconsistency, reported in notes. No fields carry a trailing '*' in the doc, so none are marked mandatory (required=false for all). The 'Possible value' column is blank for all fields except the 't' task discriminators (p/pk/pm/up/upk).
